scenario: A community newsletter receives an image with a valid-looking content credential, but the caption claims it shows a specific local event.

credential_signal.issuer_or_tool: Synthetic camera/app credential in demo fixture

what_it_supports: The file appears to have an inspectable origin/edit history in this synthetic example
The credential may help separate original capture from later screenshot/repost copies
what_it_does_not_prove: It does not prove the caption is true
It does not prove consent, fairness, or newsworthiness
It does not prove the image was taken at the claimed event
context_checks: Check event date/location against primary organizer page
Ask source how they obtained the file
Do not reuse a screenshot as if credentials survived
Verify caption separately from image origin
audience_safe_language: This image includes inspectable provenance information for the file we reviewed, but the caption and event context were checked separately.
next_review_step: Confirm the event context before publication; if context cannot be confirmed, label as illustrative or do not use.
boundary_note: Local interpretation aid only. Provenance is not truth; absence is not fakery; valid credentials do not prove consent, context, fairness, or accuracy.
